_README: Real Tank Mix T1-T8 copy from 03-execution/01-facebook-posting-playbook.md. Brackets like [state] are the only things you swap per post (edit right in the Daily Queue). Link is NEVER in the body: each template ends with a comment-keyword CTA; when someone comments the keyword you reply with the Tank Mix link + DM. Audience buckets: operators, farmer_operators, drone_curious, specialty. Image types: hero, rate_card, quote_card, screenshot. Add more hook variants per your playbook's A/B workflow.

templates.name: The Rate Question (highest engagement)
variants:
Question for the room. What's drone spray going for in your area this year?

Pulled the latest numbers from this week and the regional spread is wider than I expected:
- Corn Belt: $12 to $18
- Delta: $15 to $21
- California: $18 to $35
- Mountain West: $10 to $22

What are you guys actually charging or actually paying? Curious what's holding above $15 and what's dropping.

(I track this every Thursday in a free weekly. Reply "rates" and I'll send the full breakdown.)

Real talk on per-acre rates. What's drone spray actually going for where you are right now?

The spread this week is wider than people think:
- Corn Belt: $12 to $18
- Delta: $15 to $21
- California: $18 to $35
- Mountain West: $10 to $22

Who's holding above $15, and where is it sliding? Curious what your area looks like.

(I put the full breakdown out every Thursday in a free weekly. Reply "rates" and I'll send it.)

templates.name: The Photo Story
variants:
Mixed a hot load at 4am to catch the window before the wind came up. T50 ran clean, 22 acres in 47 minutes, no skips.

[Insert real photo: drone on tailgate at dawn, or mid-spray, or empty tank after job]

The thing they don't tell you: the run was easy. The truck-and-trailer setup the night before is the actual job.

(Posting the full season log every Thursday in a free weekly. Profile link if you want in.)

Empty tank, sun's up, 160 acres done before most folks finished their coffee. T50 didn't miss a beat.

[Insert real photo: empty tank after the job, or drone mid-spray]

What nobody tells you: the flying is the easy part. Loading and moving between fields is where the day actually goes.

(Full season log every Thursday in a free weekly. Profile link if you want in.)

templates.name: The Farmer-Ask Dual Hook
variants:
Farmers, honest question. Would you hire a drone to spray your corn or soybeans for $15 an acre, or is that high in your area?

Asking because I've been tracking what operators charge and what farmers actually want to pay, and the gap isn't where I thought it'd be.

What's the number that makes you call the guy with the drone instead of the co-op?

(Putting this whole breakdown out free on Thursday. Comment "yes" and I'll add you to the list.)

Farmers, straight question. At $15 an acre to have a drone spray your beans, is that a deal or too rich for your area?

I've been tracking what operators charge against what farmers will actually pay, and the gap surprised me.

What's the price that gets you to call the drone guy instead of the co-op?

(Putting the whole breakdown out free Thursday. Comment "yes" and I'll add you to the list.)

templates.name: The Tailgate / Send-it-to-a-buddy
variants:
Found out the hard way this week: dust on the LiDAR sensor reads as terrain. Drone "followed" the dust cloud and dropped 4 feet. Cleared a soybean canopy by inches.

Wipe your sensors. That's the post.

(More short ones like this every Thursday. Profile link.)

Quick one from this week: checked the nozzles before takeoff and caught one half-clogged. Two minutes there saved a whole reflight.

Check your nozzles cold. That's the post.

(More short ones like this every Thursday. Profile link.)

templates.name: The Job Lead / Open Acres
variants:
Open acres post.

Have a farmer in [state] looking for 380 acres of fungicide on R3 soybeans before [date]. Wants a drone, not a plane.

If you're licensed in [state] and can take the job, DM me and I'll connect you.

(I post the live job board free every Thursday. Reply "acres" and I'll add you.)

Open acres.

Farmer in [state] needs 380 acres of fungicide on R3 beans before [date]. Wants a drone, not a plane.

Licensed in [state] and able to take it? DM me and I'll put you two together.

(Live job board goes out free every Thursday. Reply "acres" and I'll add you.)

templates.name: The Honest Gear Take
variants:
T40 vs T50 vs Hylio AG-272: blunt take from guys actually flying them.

T40: cheapest to enter, tank too small for big fields, battery economy is fine
T50: 50% bigger payload, real workhorse, the battery costs eat you
Hylio AG-272: NDAA compliant which matters more every month, swarm-fly capable, support thinner

The one nobody talks about: parts wait time. DJI is 3 to 4 weeks. Hylio is 10 days but you pay for it.

(Full teardown with my own numbers in the weekly. Comment "gear" and I'll send.)

Honest gear take: T40 vs T50 vs Hylio AG-272, from guys actually flying them.

T40: cheapest way in, tank's small for big fields, battery economy is fine
T50: bigger payload, real workhorse, batteries cost you
Hylio AG-272: NDAA compliant which matters more every month, swarm capable, thinner support

The one nobody mentions: parts wait. DJI runs 3 to 4 weeks. Hylio is 10 days but you pay for it.

(Full teardown with my own numbers in the weekly. Comment "gear" and I'll send.)
